all.en: Saturday morning began with Dad announcing his ambitious plan to clean out the attic.
all.zh: 星期六早晨，爸爸宣布了他清理閣樓的雄心計畫。

all.en: The whole family groaned, but everyone knew they couldn't escape this dusty mission.
all.zh: 全家人一起哀號，但大家都知道逃不了這場灰塵任務。

all.en: Mom climbed the wooden ladder first, sneezing the moment she opened the trapdoor.
all.zh: 媽媽先爬上木梯，一打開閣樓門就忍不住打了個噴嚏。

all.en: Boxes of old photographs, broken toys, and forgotten clothes filled every corner.
all.zh: 舊照片、壞掉的玩具和被遺忘的衣服堆滿了每個角落。

all.en: Sarah discovered her childhood diary and immediately sat down to read it.
all.zh: 莎拉發現了她小時候的日記，立刻坐下來翻閱。

all.en: "Hey, no slacking off!" her brother Tom shouted while carrying a heavy box downstairs.
all.zh: 「喂，不准偷懶！」她哥哥湯姆扛著一個重箱子下樓時喊道。

all.en: They created three piles: keep, donate, and throw away, but the first pile grew the fastest.
all.zh: 他們分成三堆：保留、捐贈和丟棄，但保留那一堆卻長得最快。

all.en: Dad found his old electric guitar and started playing a terribly out-of-tune song.
all.zh: 爸爸找到了他的舊電吉他，開始彈一首走音得很離譜的歌。

all.en: Mom rolled her eyes and reminded him that they were supposed to be working.
all.zh: 媽媽翻了個白眼，提醒他他們應該在工作才對。

all.en: By lunchtime, they had filled six bags for donation and uncovered countless memories.
all.zh: 到午餐時，他們已經裝了六袋要捐贈的東西，也挖出了無數的回憶。

all.en: They ordered pizza and ate it sitting on the attic floor, surrounded by their past.
all.zh: 他們訂了披薩，坐在閣樓地板上吃著，被自己的過往環繞著。

all.en: Tom admitted that this was actually the most fun he'd had with his family in months.
all.zh: 湯姆承認這其實是他幾個月來和家人最開心的一次了。

all.en: After lunch, Sarah found a dusty wedding album from her parents' early years.
all.zh: 午餐後，莎拉找到了一本父母年輕時佈滿灰塵的婚禮相簿。

all.en: Mom blushed when the kids pointed at her huge eighties hairstyle in the photos.
all.zh: 當孩子們指著照片裡她巨大的八零年代髮型時，媽媽臉紅了。

all.en: Dad insisted that his old guitar was still valuable and refused to put it in the donation pile.
all.zh: 爸爸堅持他的舊吉他還很有價值，拒絕把它放進捐贈那堆。

all.en: Tom suggested they hold a small garage sale next weekend to make some extra money.
all.zh: 湯姆建議下週末他們辦一場小型的車庫拍賣賺點外快。

all.en: Everyone agreed, and Sarah started designing colorful price tags on her tablet.
all.zh: 大家都同意了，莎拉開始用平板設計色彩繽紛的價格標籤。

all.en: By late afternoon, the attic looked twice as big and much easier to walk around in.
all.zh: 到了傍晚，閣樓看起來大了兩倍，走動也輕鬆許多。

all.en: They installed a new ceiling light so the space wouldn't feel so gloomy anymore.
all.zh: 他們裝了一盞新的吸頂燈，這樣空間就不會再那麼陰暗。

all.en: Mom proposed turning the empty corner into a small reading nook for the family.
all.zh: 媽媽提議把空出來的角落改造成全家的小型閱讀角。

all.en: Tom carried up an old armchair, while Sarah added cushions and a soft lamp.
all.zh: 湯姆扛上一張舊扶手椅，莎拉則擺上抱枕和一盞柔和的檯燈。

all.en: That evening, Dad sat in the new reading corner and quietly admitted he loved how the attic looked.
all.zh: 那晚，爸爸坐進新的閱讀角，悄悄承認他很喜歡閣樓現在的樣子。

all.en: The following weekend, Tom and Sarah held the small garage sale they had planned together.
all.zh: 下個週末，湯姆和莎拉一起辦了他們先前計畫好的小型車庫拍賣。

all.en: Neighbors came by all morning, surprised at how many treasures the family had stored above their heads.
all.zh: 整個上午鄰居陸續上門，驚訝這家人竟在頭頂上方藏了這麼多寶物。

all.en: One older gentleman offered Dad a fair price for the dusty electric guitar from the eighties.
all.zh: 一位年長的紳士願意以合理的價錢買下那把八零年代的舊電吉他。

all.en: Dad hesitated for a long moment, but finally smiled and agreed to let the instrument go.
all.zh: 爸爸猶豫了好一會兒，最後笑著答應讓那把樂器離開。

all.en: By the end of the sale, they had earned enough money for a small family dinner downtown.
all.zh: 拍賣結束時，他們賺到的錢足以到市區享用一頓小型的家庭晚餐。

all.en: Sarah suggested they spend part of the profit on a new soft rug for the reading nook.
all.zh: 莎拉建議拿一部分收益去買一張新的軟地毯，放在閱讀角。

all.en: They visited a nearby furniture shop the next day and picked a warm cream-colored rug together.
all.zh: 隔天他們去了附近的家具行，一起挑了一張溫暖的米白色地毯。

all.en: Mom added a small wooden side table for her tea, completing the cozy corner she had imagined.
all.zh: 媽媽加上一張小木邊桌放茶杯，完成了她心中那個溫馨角落的構想。

all.en: Tom surprised everyone by spending each evening reading novels in the attic instead of gaming.
all.zh: 湯姆讓大家驚訝的是，他每晚都待在閣樓看小說，而不是打電動。

all.en: The attic, once dark and forgotten, had truly become the heart of their family weekends.
all.zh: 曾經陰暗被遺忘的閣樓，如今真正成了他們週末家庭生活的核心。
